content.text: Why Sign-Off Needs More Than Approval

'Approved' is not a decision; it is a word. A useful sign-off record states who approved, what they were approving, what criteria had to be met for them to approve, and what they are accountable for if the feature causes harm after launch. Without that specificity, sign-off becomes a rubber stamp that provides no protection and no accountability.
RACI defines who acts on each decision, not just who attends the meeting. Accountability without responsibility leads to blame; responsibility without accountability leads to orphaned decisions.

For Claude experiences this is especially important because the failure modes are not all technical — they include user harm from hallucination, bias, privacy violation, and scope drift. Different stakeholders are qualified to assess different failure modes, and the sign-off structure must reflect that.
The RACI Matrix for Claude Feature Sign-Off

attrs.alt: RACI matrix: rows are sign-off decisions, columns are stakeholder roles, cells show R/A/C/I assignments

content.text: The matrix has one Accountable owner per decision row. Accountability means the stakeholder's name is on the record and they are the escalation point if the decision is later found to be wrong. Responsible means they did the work that the decision is based on. Consulted means their input was required before the decision could be made. Informed means they are notified of the outcome.
Sign-Off Criteria Per Stakeholder
Product Manager
The PM is Accountable for two decisions: behaviour spec approval and prototype evidence review. Their sign-off criteria are:
Behaviour spec covers all requirements from the PRD with no gaps
Every must-do item has a test_signal that can be used in an automated eval
User testing was conducted with representative participants, not just the product team
Session findings were synthesised and at least one prompt revision resulted
The traceability matrix links every PRD requirement to a build ticket
Engineering Lead
The Engineering Lead is Accountable for the eval plan and operational readiness, and Responsible for final sign-off execution. Their sign-off criteria are:
Eval suite covers every must-do item in the behaviour spec
Pass rate thresholds are set and justified from prototype testing data
The eval runner is automated and runs in CI on every commit to the prompt
Observability hooks for latency, error rate, and refusal rate are wired
Rollback procedure is documented and has been tested
Rate limiting and cost caps are configured and reviewed
Legal / Compliance
Legal is Responsible for the risk and ethics review, specifically the regulatory and liability dimensions. Their sign-off criteria are:
Data retention and PII handling policy is documented and meets applicable regulations
If the feature operates in a regulated domain (health, finance, legal advice), explicit approval has been obtained
Terms of service have been reviewed for any AI-specific disclosure requirements
The failure mode registry has been reviewed for liability exposure
Security Lead
Security is Responsible for the risk and ethics review (security dimensions) and prompt design review (injection / jailbreak surface). Their sign-off criteria are:
Prompt injection attack surface has been assessed and hardening applied
Sensitive information that could be exposed through the conversation has been identified
Authentication and authorisation controls on the API endpoint are reviewed
The system prompt does not contain secrets, credentials, or internal system names
AI / ML Lead
The AI/ML Lead is Responsible for eval plan quality and prompt design review. Their sign-off criteria are:
Eval methodology is sound — test cases are representative and not cherry-picked
The system prompt is well-structured, with clear role, constraints, and output format
Token budget has been calculated and the prompt leaves sufficient context headroom
The model version is pinned and the team has a model upgrade policy
There are no known model-specific failure modes for this use case that are unmitigated
Generating the Sign-Off Record with Claude
A sign-off record is a structured document, not a meeting note. Generate it programmatically so it is queryable, auditable, and can be checked by CI.
Escalation Paths When Sign-Off is Blocked
A blocked sign-off is not a failure state — it is the process working correctly. The escalation path depends on which stakeholder is blocking and what their objection is.
Blocking stakeholder
Common reason
Escalation path
SLA
Product Manager
Behaviour spec gaps
PM revisits PRD with Engineering; re-runs spec audit
3 business days
Engineering Lead
Eval plan incomplete
AI/ML Lead provides eval methodology support
2 business days
Legal / Compliance
Regulatory gap
PM escalates to General Counsel; may require legal waiver
5 business days
Security Lead
Prompt injection risk
Engineering implements hardening; Security re-reviews
3 business days
AI / ML Lead
Eval methodology unsound
PM + Eng redesign eval suite; AI/ML Lead approves new design
3 business days
Storing Sign-Off Records
Store sign-off records as JSON files in the repository alongside the feature's behaviour spec and eval plan. This makes them auditable, version-controlled, and checkable by CI.

design-reviews/
  feature-support-escalation/
    sign-off-record.json        ← generated by create_sign_off_record()
    behaviour-spec.json
    eval-plan.json
    traceability-matrix.json
    prototype-scripts/
      happy-path.txt
      boundary.txt
      negative.txt
      recovery.txt
Common Sign-Off Anti-Patterns
Single-person sign-off
: one person cannot have deep expertise in behaviour spec quality, legal compliance, security posture, and operational readiness simultaneously. RACI exists to prevent this.
Verbal sign-off
: 'the team discussed it in the meeting' is not a sign-off record. The record must be written, dated, and committed to the repository.
Sign-off without criteria
: 'I approve' means nothing without specifying what criteria were met. The record must list the criteria checked.
Skipping sign-off under deadline pressure
: the features most likely to cause harm are the ones that bypass review. A blocked sign-off is information, not an obstacle.
Conditions that are never checked
: APPROVED_WITH_CONDITIONS means the conditions must be verified before build begins, not after launch.
Summary

Stakeholder sign-off for a Claude feature requires six decisions, each with a clearly designated Accountable owner: behaviour spec approval (PM), eval plan sign-off (Engineering Lead), risk and ethics review (Legal/Security), prompt design review (Security/AI ML Lead), prototype evidence review (PM/Designer), and operational readiness (Engineering Lead). Each owner has specific, verifiable criteria that must be met before they can approve. Sign-off records are generated programmatically, stored as JSON in the repository, and checked by CI. Blocked sign-offs follow a structured escalation path with named owners and SLAs. Verbal approval, single-person review, and approval without written criteria are anti-patterns that defeat the purpose of the sign-off gate.
